Description:
Hierarchy / linkage:
- Issue type: Task
- Task: TGTM-1499 — Build internal talking points for sales and leadership
- Parent Story: TGTM-183 — Create market correction risk messaging
- Grandparent Epic: TGTM-18 — Operationalize GTM risk mitigations
- Related sibling work under the same Story: Prepare escalation guidance for adverse market developments; Create a messaging approval and review workflow; Define trigger conditions for updating market-risk messaging; Create approved disclaimers and cautionary language; Develop an FAQ and objection-handling guide; Draft risk-aware customer and partner message variants

Original intent:
Equip frontline teams and executives with concise, consistent talking points so they can address market-correction risk confidently and avoid overpromising.

How we are doing this:
Execute this Task as part of the “Create market correction risk messaging” risk mitigation workstream. The work should translate the Task title into a usable GTM control, artifact, or operating procedure that supports ensure market-volatility conversations are credible, calm, compliant, and consistent across sales, leadership, marketing, partners, and investor communications. Start from the original intent, collect the relevant inputs, create the practical output, review it with the right owners, and make it usable by the teams responsible for launch execution.

Preliminary solution:
Create a one-page leadership/sales script with the approved narrative, short answers for common objections, do/don't-say guidance, and escalation language for sensitive questions.

Detailed execution approach:
- Confirm the exact business scenario this Task controls within the parent Story: Create market correction risk messaging.
- Collect required inputs: Dubai real estate market data, pricing/transaction trends, investor concerns, approved legal disclaimers, competitor claims, sales objections, and leadership narrative.
- Identify the primary user of the output, such as sales, marketing, leadership, compliance, investor relations, partnerships, support, or product operations.
- Draft the artifact in a practical format: checklist, matrix, register, playbook, FAQ, workflow, runbook, messaging guide, or monitoring log depending on the Task intent.
- Separate approved language/actions from prohibited or escalation-required language/actions.
- Map every high-risk statement or decision to evidence, owner, approver, and review cadence.
- Review the draft with the parent Story owner and any required legal/compliance, product, marketing, sales, or leadership stakeholders.
- Publish the final artifact in the shared GTM repository and link the artifact back to this Jira item.

Expected deliverable:
Sales and leadership talking-points sheet plus a short enablement brief.

Implementation notes:
- Keep the output specific to the parent Story so it does not become a generic policy document.
- Use plain language and operational examples so frontline teams can apply it without interpretation.
- Where the Task touches external-facing claims, include approved wording, restricted wording, evidence requirements, and escalation rules.
- Where the Task touches operations or incident response, include owner, trigger, severity, SLA, response step, and follow-up requirement.
- Where the Task touches monitoring, define the signal source, review cadence, threshold, responsible owner, and action required.

Dependencies / inputs:
- Parent Story objective: Create market correction risk messaging.
- GTM materials and launch plan relevant to this risk area.
- Product/platform facts and operational constraints that affect what can be promised externally.
- Legal/compliance guidance for claims, investor rights, liquidity, custody/security, advertising, and regional restrictions where applicable.
- Sales, marketing, investor relations, partnerships, support, and leadership feedback on likely objections or execution risks.

Acceptance criteria:
- The deliverable directly supports the parent Story and is linked in Jira.
- The output includes owner, approver, input sources, review cadence, and escalation path where relevant.
- Approved and prohibited language/actions are clearly separated where the Task involves communications or claims.

Summary: Operationalize GTM risk mitigations
Description:
Hierarchy / linkage:
- Issue type: Epic
- Epic: TGTM-18 — Operationalize GTM risk mitigations
- Child Stories in this extract: TGTM-183 Create market correction risk messaging; TGTM-182 Create operational readiness risk mitigation plan; TGTM-181 Create cybersecurity/custody trust risk mitigation plan; TGTM-180 Create paid media restriction risk mitigation plan; TGTM-179 Create competition risk mitigation plan; TGTM-178 Create investor education risk mitigation plan; TGTM-177 Create developer adoption risk mitigation plan; TGTM-176 Create trust risk mitigation plan; TGTM-175 Create liquidity risk mitigation plan; TGTM-174 Create regulatory claims risk register

Original intent:
Convert the regulatory, liquidity, trust, developer adoption, education, competition, paid media, cybersecurity/custody, operational, and market-correction risks into controls and monitoring tasks.

How we are doing this:
Operationalize GTM risk mitigation as a cross-functional control system rather than a one-time document. The Epic should coordinate risk work across regulatory claims, liquidity, trust, developer adoption, investor education, competition, cybersecurity/custody, paid media restrictions, operational readiness, and market-correction messaging. Each Story should convert a risk category into a practical operating package: approved language, owner accountability, review workflow, trigger conditions, evidence requirements, escalation path, and acceptance criteria.

Preliminary solution:
Create a GTM Risk Mitigation Operating Pack with four layers: (1) a master risk register identifying each GTM risk, owner, severity, likelihood, mitigation, evidence, and review cadence; (2) approved messaging and claim guardrails for website, pitch deck, sales, paid media, investor onboarding, developer communication, and partner communication; (3) escalation and incident response workflows for market volatility, operational launch issues, ad restrictions, trust/scam concerns, cybersecurity/custody issues, and regulatory-claim concerns; and (4) reporting routines so leadership can review open risks, blocked approvals, unresolved claims, and emerging signals before launch decisions are made.

Expected deliverable:
A complete GTM risk management workstream that can be imported into Jira, assigned to owners, and executed as launch-readiness work. Final outputs should include the master risk register, risk-specific playbooks, approval workflows, disclosure/claim libraries, stakeholder FAQs, escalation matrices, monitoring logs, and leadership review summary.

Dependencies / inputs:
- GTM calendar, campaign plan, website/pitch/sales materials, investor onboarding flow, developer outreach materials, paid media plan, and launch runbook.
- Legal/compliance input on claims, ownership, token rights, custody, liquidity, advertising restrictions, and jurisdiction-specific statements.
- Product/platform input on actual capabilities, custody model, onboarding journey, support capacity, investor servicing, and release readiness.
- Sales, marketing, partnerships, investor relations, and leadership input on customer objections, competitive narratives, market concerns, and high-risk communications.

Implementation notes:
- Treat each risk category as a live operating control with an owner and review cadence, not as a static policy memo.
- Any external-facing claim must be evidence-backed, approved, and traceable to the risk register or approved messaging library.
- High-risk items should have escalation triggers and a named decision owner before campaign launch.
- Risk outputs should be reusable by SDRs, founders, marketing, compliance, support, investor relations, and partner teams.

Acceptance criteria:
- All child Stories have defined owners, deliverables, risk controls, and acceptance criteria.
- A master risk register exists and maps risks to mitigations, owners, evidence, status, review cadence, and escalation path.
- Approved messaging, claim guardrails, and disclosure language are available for all high-risk GTM surfaces.
- Launch, paid media, investor education, developer adoption, trust, liquidity, market correction, custody/security, competition, and regulatory-claims risks each have executable mitigation plans.
- Leadership can review open risks and make go/no-go decisions using the resulting Jira items and supporting artifacts.
